scholarly_consensus: Attic Classical core from Allen Vox Graeca + metrics/inscriptions/grammarians; high on stops/vowels/quantity/pitch; Koine shifts well-attested via papyri.

pronunciation_variants.attic: 5th‚Äď4th BCE: aspirated stops [p į t į k į], voiced stops [b d g], /y/ upsilon, pitch accent, full quantity.
pronunciation_variants.koine: Hellenistic: spirantization begins (/p į/‚Üí/f/ etc.), iotacism starts, h lost.
pronunciation_variants.byzantine: Medieval: full stress accent, iotacism complete, Modern-like vowels/fricatives.

vowels: Short: /a e i y o/; Long: /aňź …õňź eňź iňź yňź …Ēňź uňź/ (+ diphthongs ai/au/eu/oi etc.; ei/ou monophthongize early). Quantity moraic.
consonants: Stops: /p t k p į t į k į b d g/; Fricatives /s h/; Nasals /m n/; Liquids /r (trilled, initial voiceless possible) l/; Semivowels /j w/ (lost in Attic text). Zeta [zd] or [dz] debate.
phoneme_inventory.quantity_system: Phonemic length (vowels + geminates); morae for accent/meter.
phoneme_inventory.distinctive_features: Three-way stop contrast; rough/smooth breathing (h vs zero); pitch accent phonemic.

ipa_reconstructed.confidence: high (85‚Äď90% core consonants/vowels/quantity; 70% exact pitch contour)
ipa_reconstructed.confidence_note: Vowels/stops/quantity strongly attested; pitch via grammarians (reconstructed melodic); Homeric w/digamma metrical but text-silent; zeta/iotacism timing disputed.

stress_rules: Pitch accent: one high-tone mora per word (acute = high/rising; circumflex = falling on long; grave = default/low or sandhi).

prosody.pitch: Musical contour (high tone on accented mora); practical recreation as slight rise/fall.
prosody.pacing: Longs/diphthongs double mora; aspirates breathy release.
prosody.pitch_accent_note: Not English stress; changes to stress accent post-Classical.

grok_imagine_guidance: Strong breathy puff on aspirates (phi= p+h not f; theta t+h; chi k+h); upsilon rounded [y] (French tu); beta pure [b]; hold longs/diphthongs 2√ó duration; musical pitch contour on accents (sing rising/falling); trilled rho; pure vowels (no English diphthong creep); rough breathing initial [h]; timing for hexameter flow with caesura breaths.
tutoring_hooks: Phi/Theta/Chi = aspirated stops (puff of air after p/t/k) ‚ÄĒ not modern f/th/ch sounds in Attic!
Pitch accent = musical tones (high/fall), not stress ‚ÄĒ like singing!
Eta ő∑ = long open […õňź] (evolves to i later via iotacism).
Zeta ő∂ often [zd] in Attic (like 'wisdom' z).
